title: Energy Optimisation
tagline: Reduce energy consumption through AI-driven chiller and HVAC setpoint optimisation
overview: Chillers are the single largest energy consumer in a hotel, representing 40–55% of the total electricity bill. Yet setpoints are set manually — the same value for a quiet Tuesday morning as a hot, fully-booked weekend. This solution uses five data streams from existing building systems to recommend precise, time-slot specific chiller setpoints that match actual conditions rather than assumptions.

input.summary: Five data streams collected automatically from existing building systems — no new instrumentation required.

sources.name: Building Management System (BMS)
sources.description: Real-time chiller operating parameters — current setpoint, compressor load, supply and return water temperatures.

sources.name: Property Management System (PMS)
sources.description: Live and forecast occupancy data by room type and time slot. The primary demand signal for the model.

sources.name: Weather Forecast API
sources.description: Hourly temperature, humidity, and heat-index forecasts for the next 24 hours. Drives the external heat load calculation.

sources.name: Smart Metering
sources.description: 15-minute interval energy consumption data. Used to measure baseline and validate savings after each recommendation.

sources.name: Historical Operations Log
sources.description: 12+ months of past setpoints, occupancy, and consumption. Used to train the baseline consumption model.

model.summary: Four sequential stages transform today's conditions into a single precise setpoint recommendation.

stages.name: Day Type Classification
stages.description: Classifies today's occupancy and weather profile against historical patterns to select the right baseline.

stages.name: Baseline Consumption Forecast
stages.description: Predicts expected energy draw under current conditions without any setpoint change — the counterfactual.

stages.name: Setpoint Range Analysis
stages.description: Tests the feasible setpoint range (±3°C from current) against comfort constraints and equipment limits.

stages.name: Optimisation Engine
stages.description: Selects the setpoint that minimises predicted energy consumption while maintaining guest comfort thresholds.

output.summary: Once per time slot — six times a day — a clear actionable alert is issued to the plant operator.
output.delivery: WhatsApp alert to Plant Operator + email summary to Chief Engineer
output.action: Engineer adjusts chiller setpoint on BMS control panel. Under two minutes to implement.
output.result: 10% reduction in chiller energy consumption across 120 live pilot days, controlled by occupancy and weather. Approximately 5% reduction in total hotel energy bill.

related.title: Fresh Procurement Optimisation
related.reason: Similar demand forecasting and buffer logic applied to fresh food supply chains

related.title: Predictive Maintenance
related.reason: Complements energy optimisation — reduces equipment failure that drives energy spikes

related.title: Dynamic Room Pricing
related.reason: Occupancy forecasts from this model feed directly into energy setpoint decisions
